Title: Light regulation of terpenoid biosynthesis, accumulation, and chemical diversity in plants

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use light-regulated upstream isoprenoid-pathway genes as engineering levers to increase terpenoid precursor supply; combine with downstream pathway genes and regulators for organ-specific enhancement under controlled-environment agriculture. Because this is a review, deployment should be species- and organ-specific and validated under target light spectra.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modulates precursor supply for terpenoid biosynthesis and is repeatedly associated with increased terpenoid accumulation under favorable light conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review-synthesized evidence from overexpression, transcript profiling, proteomics, and metabolite profiling across species.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light spectrum, intensity, photoperiod, DLI; especially white or blue light in several species.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light responsive in photosynthetic tissues and terpenoid-producing organs; exact pattern varies by species.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Not specifically reported in this review
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Artemisia annua leaves (no accession reported); proteomics in Cannabis sativa (no accession reported)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as part of the light-regulated upstream MEP pathway in Arabidopsis thaliana, Antirrhinum majus, Artemisia annua, Ginkgo biloba, Oncidium orchids, and Nicotiana benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing precursor supply may alter multiple terpenoid branches and photosynthesis-related isoprenoids, causing off-target metabolic shifts.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast (general enzyme localization for MEP pathway)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS is repeatedly described as a light-regulated upstream terpenoid-pathway gene. 2. Overexpression of DXS can increase artemisinin levels by increasing precursor supply. 3. White or blue light promotes expression of this upstream section in Artemisia annua and other species.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, fruits, flowers, rhizomes, glandular trichomes, secretory structures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aroma, flavor, color, nutritional value, medicinal bioactivity, stress protection

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for increasing fruit carotenoids through light management or gene stacking with photoreceptor/HY5 modules; useful for improving nutritional quality, especially lycopene and β-carotene, in preharvest and postharvest systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first committed step of carotenoid biosynthesis by condensing geranylgeranyl diphosphate molecules to form phytoene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lycopene and β-carotene accumulation in tomato fruits under red/blue light signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Light-treatment studies correlated with gene expression and carotenoid accumulation; rate-determinant role discussed from prior functional studies.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Committed-step enzyme supplying the first dedicated carotenoid intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High red:far-red ratio; red and blue light; postharvest and preharvest light treatments.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in tomato fruit under high R:FR and blue-light-associated signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as an enzymatic rate determinant in carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not specifically reported; broad carotenoid changes may alter fruit color-development balance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate determinant/key control step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY expression is induced under high R:FR via PHY/PIF/HY5 signaling. 2. High PSY expression is associated with high lycopene accumulation in tomato fruit. 3. PSY is presented as a central rate-determining carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme under light regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional antioxidant, health-promoting compound, fruit color quality

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target to enhance upstream precursor flux toward artemisinin when stacked with ADS/CYP71AV1 and light-responsive regulators under blue/white light regimes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate to MEP-pathway intermediate via reductoisomerization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression contributes to higher artemisinin levels by boosting upstream precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression evidence and light-responsive expression studies summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoted by white or blue light in Artemisia annua.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Artemisia annua leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of an entire light-regulated upstream set of MEP-pathway enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly affect plastidial isoprenoids beyond artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control point suggested, not uniquely established as sole bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXR belongs to the light-regulated upstream MEP pathway. 2. Overexpression of DXR can increase artemisinin accumulation. 3. White or blue light promotes its expression in Artemisia annua.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for boosting upstream flux toward artemisinin in combination with DXS, DXR, FPS, ADS, and CYP71AV1 under optimized blue/white light conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 4-hydroxy-3-methylbut-2-enyl diphosphate to IPP/DMAPP end products of the MEP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased artemisinin biosynthesis by enhancing plastidial isoprenoid precursor output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression evidence and transcript regulation under light summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light promoted in Artemisia annua under white or blue light.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Artemisia annua leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of the light-regulated upstream MEP genes across several plants.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can change pools for many plastid-derived terpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDR is a light-regulated upstream MEP-pathway enzyme. 2. Overexpression can increase artemisinin levels. 3. Blue/white light promotes expression of this precursor-supply module.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A core engineering target for artemisinin enhancement; stack with precursor-supply genes and blue-light/HY5/GSW1/ORA modules. Best deployed in glandular-trichome-rich tissues and controlled-light systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl diphosphate to amorpha-4,11-diene, the first dedicated intermediate toward artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes artemisinin production; blue light significantly increases ADS expression correlating with higher artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression under light treatments; pathway assignment; transgenic/regulatory studies summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms dedicated committed intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoted by blue or white light in Artemisia annua leaves/glandular trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Artemisia annua leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the most central downstream pathway genes for artemisinin assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux diversion may reduce other sesquiterpenes; no explicit growth penalty reported here.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/committed-step enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaADS is essential for the downstream dedicated branch of artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. Blue light significantly induces AaADS expression and correlates with increased artemisinin production. 3. HY5-centered blue-light signaling promotes AaADS through glandular trichome regulators.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stack with ADS and upstream precursor genes to improve artemisinin yield; consider blue/white/red light dependence and trichome-targeted regulatory modules.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase catalyzing oxidation steps downstream of amorpha-4,11-diene toward artemisinin intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports artemisinin biosynthesis; expression is promoted by blue/white/red depending on study context, though blue effects may be weaker than for ADS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Light-responsive expression analyses and pathway mapping summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms downstream intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue, white, or red light depending on experimental context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoted by B/W/R light in Artemisia annua according to cited studies; response context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Artemisia annua leaves (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One cited study found blue light significantly promoted ADS but not CYP71AV1, indicating differential light responsiveness within the same pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential context-dependent bottlenecks and oxidative-metabolism burdens not resolved in review.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream enzyme, bottleneck status context dependent
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaCYP71AV1 is a core downstream oxidative enzyme in artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. Its expression is promoted by light, though responses differ among experiments. 3. It acts downstream of HY5-linked blue-light signaling together with AaADS and AaORA.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhance FPP supply for artemisinin by combining FPS overexpression with upstream MEP genes and downstream ADS/CYP71AV1 under favorable light regimes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes farnesyl diphosphate from isoprenoid precursors for sesquiterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression can increase artemisinin levels by increasing FPP precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White or blue light promotes upstream section generally; specific FPS light response implied in Artemisia context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light-promoted upstream precursor pathway in Artemisia annua
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review places FPS among key upstream enzymes whose overexpression increases artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: FPP precursor supply to sesquiterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FPS supplies FPP for artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increases artemisinin levels. 3. It is part of the upstream module responsive to favorable light conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to improve fruit aroma and pathogen resistance; can be paired with red-enriched postharvest lighting or fruit-specific expression for nerolidol/linalool enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final formation of nerolidol and linalool from prenyl diphosphate substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher FaNES1 expression is associated with increased nerolidol emission under red-enriched light; relevant to fruit flavor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite correlation under light treatment; prior functional assignment of enzyme activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in strawberry fruit under red-enriched film.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Final step for biosynthesis of both nerolidol and linalool was attributed to FaNES1 in cited sources.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter aroma balance; not specifically reported.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaNES1 catalyzes the final step leading to nerolidol and linalool. 2. Red-enriched postharvest light increases FaNES1 expression. 3. Increased FaNES1 expression is consistent with higher nerolidol emission and fruit aroma modulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor, aroma, possible defense against pathogens

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential resistance-improvement target for breeding or engineering to increase germacrene D and Botrytis resistance in fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases germacrene D and improves resistance to Botrytis cinerea.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as an example of terpenoid contribution to fruit quality and defense.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter fruit flavor profile; no explicit penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for germacrene D formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaTPS1 overexpression increases germacrene D levels. 2. Higher germacrene D is associated with improved Botrytis cinerea resistance. 3. This gene illustrates how terpene engineering can improve both flavor-related metabolism and defense.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A direct target for increasing THCA in flowers; best combined with trichome-specific regulatory modules and optimized UV/B/R light cues, but careful chemotype-specific validation is needed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative cyclization converting cannabigerolic acid to Δ9-tetrahydrocannabinolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines THCA biosynthesis in flower trichomes; expression is positively regulated by trichome TF networks.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptional regulation evidence and pathway assignment summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potentially influenced indirectly by UV, blue, and red light via transcription factors; direct light effect on gene not established here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Cannabis sativa (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central pathway gene in review figure for light-regulated cannabinoid production; actual HY5 mediation remains hypothetical in cannabis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifts chemotype balance among cannabinoids may occur.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THCAS catalyzes THCA formation from CBGA in cannabis trichomes. 2. Its transcription is controlled by trichome-specific TFs CsAP2L1, CsMYB1, and CsWRKY1. 3. Light-regulated HY5/UVR8 control is proposed but not yet established directly.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point to enhance CBGA-derived cannabinoids if coordinated with MEP-pathway precursor genes and trichome regulation; still needs direct light-regulation validation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization in olivetolic acid formation within the polyketide branch leading to cannabinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports OA production and therefore CBGA formation, influencing downstream cannabinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway assignment summarized in review; light effect inferred from type III polyketide synthase responsiveness, not directly demonstrated for OAC here.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV and blue light may have inductive effects, but this remains unverified in cannabis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics in Cannabis sativa (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes OAC belongs to the type III polyketide synthase-related upstream section conceptually linked to light regulation, but this specific assignment is somewhat imprecise and requires further study.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may alter multiple cannabinoid outputs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OAC is required for olivetolic acid formation upstream of CBGA. 2. UV and blue light may influence this branch, but evidence is indirect. 3. It remains an underexplored candidate in light-regulated cannabinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: CBGA-derived cannabinoids including THCA, CBDA, CBCA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plants / mint and cannabis context
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway / monoterpene and cannabinoid precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for increasing GPP and monoterpene/cannabinoid precursor availability; useful in combination with light optimization, but species-specific branch competition must be managed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes geranyl diphosphate from isoprenoid precursors for monoterpene and cannabinoid precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream production of GPP, a precursor for monoterpenes and for CBGA synthesis in cannabis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression effects under blue/UV light reported in mint; conservation and pathway role discussed for cannabis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue and UV light in mint; inferred relevance to cannabis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light responsive in mint; not directly shown for cannabis here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as well-conserved in plants and relevant to the upstream biosynthesis of cannabis terpenoids and cannabinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced GPP supply may redistribute flux among monoterpenes, carotenoids, and cannabinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPPS supplies GPP for monoterpenes and cannabinoid biosynthesis. 2. Blue and UV light affect GPPS transcript levels in mint. 3. The enzyme is proposed to be relevant for light-regulated precursor supply in cannabis as well.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plants / mint and cannabis context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential support gene for balancing IPP/DMAPP precursor pools under light-optimized terpene production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts IPP and DMAPP to balance prenyl precursor pools for terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects upstream precursor balance linked to monoterpene and cannabinoid biosynthesis under light-responsive conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript response to blue/UV light in mint summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in review as a light-responsive upstream terpenoid gene in mint relevant by conservation to cannabis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad precursor rebalancing may affect many isoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/cytosol depending isoform, not specified here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPPi is light responsive in mint. 2. It helps maintain precursor balance for terpene biosynthesis. 3. Its conservation suggests relevance to cannabis terpenoid/cannabinoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports formation of aroma, defense, and medicinal metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/target for berry aroma enhancement by canopy light management or breeding for higher sunlight responsiveness.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces linalool and nerolidol volatile terpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression positively correlates with linalool concentration in grape berry mesocarp and exocarp after sunlight exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Berry mesocarp and exocarp under sunlight exposure
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as evidence that increased light often increases terpene synthase expression and volatile output.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvPNLinNer1 expression positively correlates with linalool levels after sunlight exposure. 2. It supports light-responsive volatile terpene formation in grape berries. 3. It exemplifies the linkage between TPS transcription and terpene output.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing β-caryophyllene production if combined with MYB/UVR8-responsive regulatory modules; underground-organ relevance remains hypothetical in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with β-caryophyllene production and cited as MYB-regulated terpene synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior transcriptional-regulation evidence cited in review.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potential UV-related regulation via MYB/UVR8 proposed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in review to support a hypothetical UVR8–MYB–βcarTPS mechanism in rhizomes of other species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DoβcarTPS is associated with β-caryophyllene production. 2. MYBs regulate this TPS in an aboveground context. 3. The review extrapolates this to propose possible UVR8/MYB control of β-caryophyllene in rhizomes.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime regulator for engineering light-responsive terpenoid enhancement; suitable for stacking with pathway genes, but due to broad photomorphogenic roles it should be deployed with tissue-specific or inducible strategies to minimize pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central positive regulator integrating UV, red, and blue light signaling to promote terpenoid biosynthesis, including carotenoids, artemisinin-pathway genes, and potentially cannabinoids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review-synthesized evidence from light treatments, gene-expression studies, transgenic photoreceptor studies, and regulatory pathway analyses.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator/integrator of light-induced terpenoid biosynthesis across multiple pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV, red, blue, far-red light; high R:FR ratio; controlled-environment light spectra
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light induced; active in fruits, leaves, flowers, and roots/underground signaling context depending species.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Multiple plant species under light-responsive conditions
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: COP1; CRYs; PHYs; UVR8; PIFs; AaGSW1; CsAP2L1; CsWRKY1 (proposed context)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and transcriptional activation of terpenoid-pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics/transcript-regulation evidence summarized; no accession numbers
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described explicitly in the highlights as playing a central role in UV, red, and blue light signaling regulating terpenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because HY5 broadly controls light development programs, manipulation may cause pleiotropic growth and morphology effects.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PSY; AaORA; AaADS; AaCYP71AV1; potentially CsAP2L1 and CsWRKY1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of photoreceptor signaling; stabilized by inhibition of COP1-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PSY and artemisinin-pathway regulators/genes; potentially promotes cannabinoid regulatory TFs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is the central integrator of UV, red, and blue light signaling regulating terpenoid biosynthesis. 2. In tomato, HY5 promotes PSY expression and carotenoid accumulation. 3. In Artemisia, HY5-centered signaling promotes AaORA, AaADS, and AaCYP71AV1. 4. In cannabis and underground organs, HY5 is proposed as a mobile/systemic integrator but direct evidence remains incomplete.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Carotenoids; artemisinin; cannabinoids and other terpenoids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional quality, medicinal activity, aroma/flavor, color, stress protection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: UVR8, CRYs, PHYs, light spectrum and R:FR ratio

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating PIF repression/degradation under high R:FR may be a route to increase carotenoids, but broad developmental effects make direct deployment risky.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulators whose degradation under red light permits higher PSY and HY5 expression and increased carotenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Light signaling framework and correlation with carotenoid accumulation described in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controllers acting as repressors released by active phytochromes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High R:FR ratio; red and far-red light balance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light signaling components in tomato fruit and other tissues
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of carotenoid biosynthesis and other light-responsive terpenoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level entry; specific PIF members were not identified in the review text.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad roles in shade avoidance and development could create major pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physical interaction with phytochromes and cryptochromes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression; degraded/inhibited under active phytochrome or cryptochrome signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PSY and HY5-associated carotenoid pathway outputs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Active PHY signaling under high R:FR promotes PIF degradation. 2. Removal of PIF repression allows increased HY5 and PSY expression. 3. This promotes tomato carotenoid accumulation, especially lycopene.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Red and far-red light via phytochromes; blue light via cryptochrome interaction

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Photoreceptor engineering or spectral control of PHY activity is valuable for increasing carotenoids and some monoterpenes without transgene edits to pathway enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active under red/high R:FR conditions, represses PIF function and enables HY5/PSY-mediated terpenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Light signaling and metabolite correlation summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major light-input controller for red/far-red regulation of carotenoids and likely monoterpenes.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Light perception controlling terpenoid biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level entry; specific PHY paralogs not identified.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Modifying PHY signaling may alter growth architecture and developmental timing.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Photoreceptor-mediated inhibition/degradation of PIFs; promotion of HY5-associated transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly activates PSY and other HY5-responsive genes by suppressing PIFs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. High R:FR increases PHY activity. 2. Active PHYs inhibit PIFs, relieving repression of HY5 and PSY. 3. This promotes carotenoid accumulation in tomato and likely related light responses in other terpenoid pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plants including tomato and Artemisia
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating CRY signaling or blue-light regimes can enhance carotenoids and artemisinin; photoreceptor-based strategies may avoid direct editing of numerous pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Blue-light photoreceptors that stabilize HY5 through COP1 interaction and promote terpenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Photoreceptor overexpression in Artemisia annua and light-signaling studies summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential blue-light controller enabling HY5-mediated enhancement of carotenoids and artemisinin.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Light perception regulating terpenoid biosynthetic gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: General family entry; in Artemisia a specific member AaCRY1 was functionally highlighted.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad blue-light developmental responses may affect morphology and growth.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HY5 stabilization pathway; indirectly PSY, AaORA, AaADS, AaCYP71AV1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Photoreceptor complexes with COP1 and PIFs
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Photoreceptor-mediated binding to COP1/PIFs preventing HY5 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect activation of carotenoid and artemisinin genes via HY5 stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Blue light induces CRY activity. 2. CRYs bind COP1 and PIFs, reducing HY5 degradation. 3. This enhances terpenoid biosynthesis including tomato carotenoids and Artemisia artemisinin-pathway genes.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A validated photoreceptor target for raising artemisinin; can be overexpressed or activated via blue-light strategies, ideally combined with downstream pathway and trichome regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases artemisinin content by about 43% relative to wild type.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in transgenic Artemisia annua plants.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key blue-light input enhancing artemisinin accumulation through HY5-centered signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the strongest direct functional validations in the review for light-signaling control of a medicinal terpenoid.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on photomorphogenesis not discussed.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AaHY5 stabilization pathway; indirectly AaGSW1, AaORA, AaADS, AaCYP71AV1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Photoreceptor-mediated stabilization of HY5 via COP1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AaCRY1 increased artemisinin content by 43%. 2. AaCRY1 enhances interaction with AaCOP1 and prevents AaHY5 degradation. 3. This promotes a glandular-trichome transcriptional cascade elevating artemisinin biosynthetic genes.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plants including tomato and Artemisia
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indirect but powerful leverage point; reducing COP1 activity in target tissues could increase HY5 and terpenoid biosynthesis, though pleiotropic photomorphogenesis effects are a major caution.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of HY5; when sequestered by CRYs/UVR8/PHY-associated signaling, HY5 is stabilized and terpenoid biosynthesis increases.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mechanistic light-signaling evidence summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong repressor node whose inhibition is necessary for HY5-dependent terpenoid induction.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Proteostasis control of light-regulated terpenoid transcriptional programs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as E3 ubiquitin-ligase CONSTITUTIVELY PHOTOMORPHOGENIC 1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad developmental defects possible if constitutively suppressed.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of photoreceptor signaling complexes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ubiquitin-mediated degradation of HY5; inhibited by photoreceptor interactions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect repression of HY5-responsive terpenoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COP1 targets HY5 for degradation. 2. Blue and UV photoreceptor signaling inhibit COP1 action. 3. HY5 stabilization downstream of COP1 inhibition promotes terpenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Multiple terpenoids including carotenoids and artemisinin

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Trichome-focused regulator suitable for tissue-specific engineering to boost artemisinin while potentially reducing whole-plant pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of HY5 to enhance expression of artemisinin-related genes under blue light.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory model synthesized from cited studies in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Trichome-associated positive regulator linking blue-light signaling to artemisinin biosynthetic activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as A. annua glandular trichome-specific regulator in the review text.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glandular trichome-specific regulator (family not specified in review)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation downstream of HY5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances AaORA and downstream AaADS/AaCYP71AV1-related pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaGSW1 acts downstream of AaHY5 in blue-light signaling. 2. It helps activate the artemisinin biosynthetic regulatory cascade in trichomes. 3. It contributes to increased expression of downstream genes such as AaORA, AaADS, and AaCYP71AV1.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising downstream regulator for trichome-targeted engineering of artemisinin production under blue-light-enriched cultivation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator associated with increased AaADS and AaCYP71AV1 expression and higher artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive regulator of the dedicated downstream artemisinin branch.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves/glandular trichomes under blue-light response context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shown in review figure as HY5-promoted under blue light.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2-like/orange-related regulator as named in review
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AaADS and AaCYP71AV1-associated pathway expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaORA is promoted by HY5-centered blue-light signaling. 2. It activates downstream artemisinin-pathway genes. 3. It is part of the glandular-trichome regulatory module enhancing artemisinin production.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong trichome-specific activator candidate for increasing THCA, especially with flower/trichome-targeted expression and compatible light management.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates THCAS transcription, promoting THCA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptional regulation evidence summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive transcriptional activator of THCAS in trichomes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potentially influenced by blue/red light via HY5, but direct light regulation not established
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes CsAP2L1 positively regulates THCAS, opposite to CsMYB1 and CsWRKY1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could shift cannabinoid profile toward THCA branch.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAP2L1 positively regulates THCAS transcription. 2. It is one of three trichome-specific TFs controlling THCA biosynthesis. 3. HY5-mediated light regulation is proposed but not yet directly demonstrated.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Potential HY5-mediated blue/red light signaling; possibly UV/HY5 context

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target for de-repression of THCAS and THCA accumulation, but broader MYB-mediated effects should be assessed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of THCAS transcription, thereby repressing THCA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptional regulation evidence summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong repressor of the THCAS branch.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potential UVR8 interaction under UV proposed; direct evidence lacking
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review proposes possible UVR8-CsMYB1 interaction but states mechanism is not established.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removing repression may alter broader MYB-controlled traits.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Potential interaction with UVR8 signaling complex (hypothetical)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB1 represses THCAS transcription. 2. It is one of the trichome-specific TFs controlling THCA biosynthesis. 3. UVR8-mediated regulation is proposed but unproven.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate repressor for editing or modulation to increase THCA; trichome-specific deployment would be preferable.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of THCAS transcription affecting THCA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptional regulation evidence summarized in review.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong repressor within trichome-specific control of THCA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potentially downstream of HY5/UV signaling in hypothetical model
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review figure proposes HY5 promotes WRKY1, but text also states CsWRKY1 negatively regulates THCAS; this suggests unresolved network complexity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing may perturb wider stress-response functions of WRKY TFs.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsWRKY1 is a trichome-specific regulator of THCAS. 2. The text states it represses THCAS, reducing THCA biosynthesis. 3. Its placement in the hypothetical HY5 model remains unresolved and needs experimental clarification.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plants including cannabis and underground organs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: UVR8-directed spectral management is promising for terpenoid and cannabinoid modulation, but dose/genotype specificity is critical and direct transcriptional targets remain incompletely mapped.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UV photoreceptor proposed to promote HY5 and MYB-mediated terpenoid/cannabinoid regulation under UV light.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Known UV signaling framework integrated with review-cited terpenoid examples; several mechanisms remain hypothetical.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent UV-specific controller of terpenoid and cannabinoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light-sensing component in aerial and near-surface underground tissues
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: UV-responsive transcriptional regulation of terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review emphasizes hypothetical UVR8–MYB and UVR8–HY5 mechanisms in cannabis and rhizomes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: UV responses can be dose dependent and can reduce metabolite levels or damage tissues under some conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HY5; MYB-associated TPS regulation; potentially cannabis cannabinoid TF modules
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Photoreceptor signaling leading to transcriptional induction via HY5/MYB interactions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Potentially activates TPS and cannabinoid regulatory genes under UV
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UVR8 is the main UV photoreceptor implicated in terpenoid regulation. 2. It can promote HY5 induction and may interact with MYB factors. 3. In cannabis and rhizomes, proposed UVR8-centered mechanisms remain hypothetical and need direct validation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Various terpenoids including cannabinoids and β-caryophyllene-related products

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MYB regulators could be exploited to increase specific sesquiterpenes such as β-caryophyllene, but target MYB identity and tissue specificity must be defined first.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participate in UV-light-related regulation of terpenoid biosynthesis and are proposed to control TPS genes such as β-caryophyllene synthases.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior studies and review-based mechanistic hypothesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controllers of specific terpene branches rather than universal master regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots/underground organs contain increasing numbers of uncharacterized MYBs; exact patterns not specified
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Specific terpene synthase transcription, branch selection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family-level review entry; specific MYBs mostly not named except cannabis CsMYB1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: MYBs often regulate broad specialized-metabolism programs, so off-target effects are possible.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation or repression depending member
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Can promote TPS expression; in cannabis CsMYB1 represses THCAS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB transcription factors are implicated in UV-light signaling affecting terpenoids. 2. They regulate β-caryophyllene TPS in Dendrobium officinale. 3. The review proposes similar UVR8–MYB–TPS regulation in rhizomes, but direct evidence is lacking.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes and other organs depending species
